page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor is equipped with advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations. In performance benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong single-core and multi-core capabilities. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2074.0 and a multi-core score of 15951.0, both falling into the strong performance tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register strong results at 33.6 GB/sec and 13.85 GB/sec respectively, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches an average tier of 111.1 GB/sec.

Memory performance is characterized by a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 2625.73 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 162.39 GB/sec. The instance delivers strong static web serving throughput at 51.12 GB/sec and a strong Passmark CPU encryption score of 35678.6. For machine learning workloads, the hardware supports small-model text generation at a strong rate of 286.26 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for the same model and text generation for medium models remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2, making it a resource-dense option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, limiting its utility for local-storage-heavy databases or large-scale deep learning training. The instance is suited for tasks such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It exhibits strong performance in single-core and multi-core benchmarks, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2074.0 and a multi-core score of 15951.0. Advanced instruction support includes AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. The instance delivers strong static web serving throughput of 51.12 GB/sec and strong single-threaded compression speeds. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads. It is suited for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and small-to-medium model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and multi-core performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for high-throughput web serving and processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C CPU
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong benchmark performance in single-core processing and static web serving
No local storage or GPU, relying on network-attached resources
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing
